title: Hades's Left-Head Visit
tagline: Hades said the left head's return would require proper documentation. The documentation has arrived. The left head is back. It brought paperwork.
hook_line: An official document arrived at the Olympus gate. It was addressed to: Archie (Owl), c/o Little Olympus. The return address was the underworld. The document inside was a Formal Request for Social Visit, subsection 3: Non-Primary Cerberus Head. Filed by: Hades, Administrator. Approved by: Hades, Administrator. Signed and counter-signed by: Hades. The left head had filled in its name field as: THE CURIOUS ONE.

scenes.title: The Official Document
scenes.narration: The document arrived in a black envelope with a gold underworld seal. It was addressed specifically to Archie. Athena signed for it. She brought it to Archie on his windowsill. He looked at the envelope for a moment. He looked at Athena. She opened it, because Archie does not have hands. She read it aloud. The Formal Request for Social Visit. Filed by, approved by, signed and counter-signed by: Hades. The applicant's name field: THE CURIOUS ONE. 'That is the left head,' Athena said. Archie slow-blinked once, which was neither confirmation nor surprise — it was Archie indicating he had registered the information. 'Hades actually filed the paperwork,' Athena said, more to herself than to Archie.
scenes.visual_prompt: The black envelope with gold underworld seal arriving at the gate — addressed specifically to Archie (Owl) — Athena opening it on Archie's windowsill — the official document: Formal Request for Social Visit, every field filled out with Hades's characteristic thoroughness — filed by/approved by/counter-signed by all Hades — the applicant name: THE CURIOUS ONE — Athena reading it aloud — Archie's single slow-blink — Athena's 'Hades actually filed the paperwork' to herself, which contains the surprise — the document's visual: all the formal underworld typography, the left head's name in the applicant field standing out — #1A0A3D and black envelope contrast, the gold seal the entry point

scenes.title: Zeus Reviews the Documentation
scenes.narration: Zeus reviewed the document. He was thorough — he read every subsection. 'This is a legitimate Formal Request,' he said. 'Yes,' Athena said. 'Hades filed his own paperwork with himself.' 'He approved it as well.' 'And counter-signed it.' Zeus set the document down. His crown listed left. 'He really wanted the left head to come back.' He paused. 'Send confirmation. The visit is approved.' Athena wrote the confirmation — an equally formal document, because you matched Hades's register when corresponding with the underworld. She signed it, had Zeus witness it, sent it back in an official envelope. 'How long does delivery to the underworld take?' Hercules asked. 'Officially,' Athena said, 'it depends on the route.' 'Unofficially?' 'Faster than you'd expect.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Zeus reading the document thoroughly — 'this is a legitimate Formal Request. He filed his own paperwork with himself. He approved it. He counter-signed it.' — crown listing left — 'he really wanted the left head to come back.' — the approval, and Athena writing the equally formal confirmation response — the formality-matching principle: you match Hades's register — Zeus witnessing the confirmation — the official black envelope going back — Hercules's question about delivery time — Athena: 'faster than you'd expect.' — Zeus's crown listed left through all of this: the specific combination of Hades-being-thorough and Hades-having-feelings that produces a crown-listing response — #1A0A3D and main hall warmth, the document exchange the formal-comedy visual

scenes.title: Hades Returns
scenes.narration: Hades arrived the next morning. He was in his formal robes again, skull clasps polished, but this time he was wearing his clear glasses — administrative mode, not departure mode. He had Cerberus on the lead. The left head was alert and pulling forward. The middle head had clearly been thinking about the Olympus kitchen for sixteen hours. The right head was asleep. 'The documentation was approved,' Hades said at the gate. 'It was,' Zeus said. 'We matched your format.' Hades looked at Zeus. 'You matched my format.' 'Athena did,' Zeus said. 'But yes.' Hades adjusted his clear glasses. Something happened in his flame — brief, warm, quickly neutralized. 'Very good,' he said.
scenes.visual_prompt: Hades at the gate: clear glasses (administrative mode), formal robes, skull clasps, Cerberus — the left head pulling forward, alert, in the direction of Archie's window — the middle head clearly thinking about the kitchen — the right head asleep — 'the documentation was approved' / 'we matched your format' — Hades's look at Zeus — 'athena did. But yes.' — the flame doing the brief warm thing that Hades quickly neutralizes — 'very good.' — Cerberus's lead at tension in three directions: left toward Archie, middle toward kitchen, right where the right head's asleep weight pulls — Zeus opening the gate — the specific moment of Hades registering that the format was matched and feeling about it — #1A0A3D and gate morning light

scenes.title: The Left Head and Archie
scenes.narration: The left head knew exactly where Archie's windowsill was. It had mapped it from the previous visit. It went directly there. Hades followed, because the left head was on the lead and was not slowing down. Archie was on the windowsill. He had been on the windowsill since the confirmation was sent — whether he had moved since then, nobody had thought to check. The left head reached the windowsill. It looked at Archie. Archie looked at it. The left head made the investigative sound it had made last time. Archie slow-blinked. The left head's tail — the whole dog's tail, not just the left head's — wagged. This woke the right head. The right head looked around, did not understand what was happening, and went back to sleep. Hades watched this and changed nothing about his expression.
scenes.visual_prompt: The left head navigating directly to Archie's windowsill — Hades being pulled there because the lead connects them — Archie on the windowsill, having been there — left head and Archie face to face again — the investigative sound — Archie's slow blink — the WHOLE DOG'S TAIL wagging: all three heads share one tail — the wag waking the right head — the right head: confused, surveying, returning to sleep — Hades watching this with complete stillness, changing nothing about his expression except that he doesn't look away — the specific quality of watching someone you care about be happy — Cerberus's three-head situation and one tail shown in full — #1A0A3D and the warm windowsill light, the tail-wag the comedy visual

scenes.title: Hades and Poseidon
scenes.narration: While the left head visited, Poseidon approached Hades. He had a question he had been holding since the Titan correspondence. 'The Titan asked,' Poseidon said, 'whether the underworld needed anything.' 'Yes,' Hades said. 'You replied that it didn't.' 'Correct.' 'But then you said you appreciated being asked.' Hades was quiet for a moment. He adjusted his clear glasses. 'Is there a question in this?' 'Yes,' Poseidon said. 'Would you like to be on the correspondence? The letters to the eastern valley and the Titan — it would be easy to include a section from the underworld.' Hades looked at Poseidon. He looked at the left head and Archie at the windowsill. He looked at his clear glasses — as if deciding something about them. He switched to his purple-tint glasses. 'I will think about it,' he said.
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon approaching Hades while the left head visits — the question about the Titan's offer and Hades's 'I appreciated being asked' — Hades's 'is there a question in this?' — Poseidon's invitation: include the underworld in the correspondence — Hades looking at the left head and Archie at the window — looking at his clear glasses — the glasses switch to purple-tint — 'I will think about it.' — the purple-tint glasses as annoyed/moved: he's moved by the invitation and annoyed that he is — 'I will think about it' as the Hades way of saying yes — Poseidon registering this and not pressing it — the sardine on his log note — the left head at the windowsill, one ear flopped toward them as if listening — #1A0A3D and the window light, the glasses switch the key beat

scenes.title: Departure Paperwork
scenes.narration: The visit reached its scheduled end — the Formal Request had specified a duration of two hours. Hades checked the time precisely. He looked at the left head, which had spent the two hours in a comfortable arrangement with Archie on the windowsill involving the left head's chin on the sill next to Archie while both of them looked out at the sea. 'It is time,' Hades said. The left head looked at him. Then back at Archie. The left head stood up — slowly — and returned to Hades's side. Archie watched it go. Hades produced a document from his robes. He gave it to Zeus. 'Visit record,' he said. 'For your files.' Zeus looked at the document. It was a Completion of Social Visit form. Hades had pre-filled it. 'You filled this out in advance,' Zeus said. 'I was optimistic,' Hades said.
scenes.visual_prompt: The two-hour mark — Hades checking the time precisely — the left head at the windowsill, chin on the sill beside Archie, both looking out at the sea — Hades: 'it is time' — the left head looking from Hades to Archie, standing slowly, returning to Hades's side — Archie watching it go — Hades producing the pre-filled Completion of Social Visit form — 'you filled this out in advance.' — 'I was optimistic.' — the specific charm of this: Hades came with the closing paperwork already filled out because he expected the visit to go well — Zeus's crown listed left — Archie on the windowsill watching the left head go — the form as the final comedy-warmth object — #1A0A3D and the departure light, the pre-filled form the punchline

scenes.title: What Hades Sends
scenes.narration: Three days later, a second black envelope arrived. Inside it was not a new Formal Request. It was a letter. Hades had written a letter. It was addressed to the eastern valley and the Titan, in care of Poseidon. It was three paragraphs. The first paragraph was about the underworld's geological position relative to the Titan's current location — subsection twelve terms — written with the same formality as his official documents. The second paragraph was about the underground river and how it sounded from below — he could hear it. The third paragraph was short: 'The river sounds like it is going somewhere, which I find encouraging. I am glad the Titan knows it is still there. — H.' Poseidon read it. He put a sardine on the envelope. 'Welcome to the correspondence,' he said.
scenes.visual_prompt: The second black envelope arriving — not a Formal Request, a letter — three paragraphs visible: the formal first (geological terms, subsection twelve), the second (he can hear the underground river from the underworld — it sounds like it's going somewhere), the third: 'I am glad the Titan knows it is still there. — H.' — Poseidon reading it at his desk — placing a sardine on the envelope — 'welcome to the correspondence.' — the correspondence now including: Poseidon, the Titan, Medusa and the left-side snakes (SEVEN, THREE, FOUR, FIVE, TWO, ONE, RIVER), and Hades — and Archie's contributions through Athena — the letter as the closing visual, Hades's three paragraphs representing three registers: formal, observational, genuine — #1A0A3D and Poseidon's desk lamp warmth, the sardine on the official black envelope the final image

viral_hook: Hades filed official paperwork so his dog's left head could visit Archie. He approved it himself. He counter-signed it. He came back with the completion form pre-filled. When Zeus said 'you filled this out in advance,' Hades said: 'I was optimistic.' Then three days later a letter arrived — Hades can hear the underground river from the underworld. 'The river sounds like it's going somewhere, which I find encouraging.'
highlight_note: Hades producing the pre-filled Completion of Social Visit form. 'You filled this out in advance.' 'I was optimistic.' The form was his entire personality in one joke.
short_title: Hades Pre-Filled the Closing Paperwork Because He Was Optimistic
rewatch_bait: Scene 5 — the glasses switch. Hades starts in clear glasses (administrative), Poseidon invites him to the correspondence, he looks at the left head and Archie, looks at his own glasses, and switches to purple-tint. Purple-tint = moved but annoyed about it. 'I will think about it' = yes.
youtube_title: Hades filed official paperwork for his dog's left head to visit an owl. Then he showed up with the closing forms pre-filled. 'I was optimistic.' | Little Olympus S2 EP16
lesson: Being formal about something doesn't mean you don't care about it. Hades filed three documents, approved them himself, and showed up with the completion paperwork already filled in. That's someone who wanted this visit to happen and prepared for it to go well. The paperwork was how he showed it mattered.
arc_notes: Hades joins the correspondence — his letter confirms he can hear the underground river from the underworld, finds it encouraging. Pre-filled completion form is the best Hades joke so far. Left-head/Archie dynamic established: two-hour windowsill visit, both looking at the sea. Cerberus one-tail detail introduced: the tail wag woke the right head, which understood nothing and went back to sleep. Poseidon-Hades correspondence dynamic: Poseidon simply included him, Hades said 'I will think about it' which Poseidon correctly reads as yes. Underworld now formally in the Titan correspondence network.
